name: openslide-python

metadata.name: openslide-python
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v2 (LGPLv2)
metadata.license: GNU Lesser General Public License, version 2.1

documentation:
# OpenSlide Python

OpenSlide Python is a Python interface to the OpenSlide library.

[OpenSlide] is a C library that provides a simple interface for reading
whole-slide images, also known as virtual slides, which are high-resolution
images used in digital pathology.  These images can occupy tens of gigabytes
when uncompressed, and so cannot be easily read using standard tools or
libraries, which are designed for images that can be comfortably
uncompressed into RAM.  Whole-slide images are typically multi-resolution;
OpenSlide allows reading a small amount of image data at the resolution
closest to a desired zoom level.

OpenSlide can read virtual slides in several formats:

* [Aperio][] (`.svs`, `.tif`)
* [DICOM][] (`.dcm`)
* [Hamamatsu][] (`.ndpi`, `.vms`, `.vmu`)
* [Leica][] (`.scn`)
* [MIRAX][] (`.mrxs`)
* [Philips][] (`.tiff`)
* [Sakura][] (`.svslide`)
* [Trestle][] (`.tif`)
* [Ventana][] (`.bif`, `.tif`)
* [Zeiss][] (`.czi`)
* [Generic tiled TIFF][] (`.tif`)

[OpenSlide]: https://openslide.org/
[Aperio]: https://openslide.org/formats/aperio/
[DICOM]: https://openslide.org/formats/dicom/
[Hamamatsu]: https://openslide.org/formats/hamamatsu/
[Leica]: https://openslide.org/formats/leica/
[MIRAX]: https://openslide.org/formats/mirax/
[Philips]: https://openslide.org/formats/philips/
[Sakura]: https://openslide.org/formats/sakura/
[Trestle]: https://openslide.org/formats/trestle/
[Ventana]: https://openslide.org/formats/ventana/
[Zeiss]: https://openslide.org/formats/zeiss/
[Generic tiled TIFF]: https://openslide.org/formats/generic-tiff/


## Requirements

* Python ≥ 3.8
* OpenSlide ≥ 3.4.0
* Pillow


## Installation

OpenSlide Python requires [OpenSlide].  Install both components from PyPI
with:

```console
pip install openslide-python openslide-bin
```

Or, see the [OpenSlide Python documentation][installing] for instructions on
installing so OpenSlide Python can find OpenSlide.

[installing]: https://openslide.org/api/python/#installing


## More Information

- [API documentation](https://openslide.org/api/python/)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/openslide/openslide-python/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md#notable-changes-in-openslide-python)
- [Website][OpenSlide]
- [GitHub](https://github.com/openslide/openslide-python)
- [Sample data](https://openslide.cs.cmu.edu/download/openslide-testdata/)


## License

OpenSlide Python is released under the terms of the [GNU Lesser General
Public License, version 2.1](https://openslide.org/license/).

OpenSlide Python is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but
WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY
or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the GNU Lesser General Public
License for more details.

Sphinx hardcodes that certain output paths have names starting with
an underscore.
Jekyll hardcodes that filenames starting with an underscore are not
deployed to the website.
Rename Sphinx output paths to drop the underscore.

modules.name: openslide.deepzoom

This module provides functionality for generating Deep Zoom images from

classes.name: openslide.deepzoom.DeepZoomGenerator

methods.name: openslide.deepzoom.DeepZoomGenerator.__init__
documentation:
Create a DeepZoomGenerator wrapping an OpenSlide object.

osr:          a slide object.
tile_size:    the width and height of a single tile.  For best viewer
              performance, tile_size + 2 * overlap should be a power
              of two.
overlap:      the number of extra pixels to add to each interior edge
              of a tile.
limit_bounds: True to render only the non-empty slide region.

methods.name: openslide.deepzoom.DeepZoomGenerator.level_count
documentation: The number of Deep Zoom levels in the image.

methods.name: openslide.deepzoom.DeepZoomGenerator.level_tiles
documentation: A tuple of (tiles_x, tiles_y) tuples for each Deep Zoom level.

methods.name: openslide.deepzoom.DeepZoomGenerator.level_dimensions
documentation: A tuple of (pixels_x, pixels_y) tuples for each Deep Zoom level.

methods.name: openslide.deepzoom.DeepZoomGenerator.tile_count
documentation: The total number of Deep Zoom tiles in the image.

methods.name: openslide.deepzoom.DeepZoomGenerator.get_tile
documentation:
Return an RGB PIL.Image for a tile.

level:     the Deep Zoom level.
address:   the address of the tile within the level as a (col, row)
           tuple.

methods.name: openslide.deepzoom.DeepZoomGenerator.get_tile_coordinates
documentation:
Return the OpenSlide.read_region() arguments for the specified tile.

Most users should call get_tile() rather than calling
OpenSlide.read_region() directly.

level:     the Deep Zoom level.
address:   the address of the tile within the level as a (col, row)
           tuple.

methods.name: openslide.deepzoom.DeepZoomGenerator.get_tile_dimensions
documentation:
Return a (pixels_x, pixels_y) tuple for the specified tile.

level:     the Deep Zoom level.
address:   the address of the tile within the level as a (col, row)
           tuple.

methods.name: openslide.deepzoom.DeepZoomGenerator.get_dzi
documentation:
Return a string containing the XML metadata for the .dzi file.

format:    the format of the individual tiles ('png' or 'jpeg')
Generates Deep Zoom tiles and metadata.

Open a whole-slide or regular image.

Return an OpenSlide object for whole-slide images and an ImageSlide
object for other types of images.

Return a string describing the format of the specified file.

If the file format is not recognized, return None.

documentation: Close the slide.

documentation: The number of levels in the image.

A tuple of (width, height) tuples, one for each level of the image.

level_dimensions[n] contains the dimensions of level n.

methods.name: openslide.AbstractSlide.dimensions
documentation: A (width, height) tuple for level 0 of the image.

methods.name: openslide.AbstractSlide.level_downsamples
documentation:
A tuple of downsampling factors for each level of the image.

level_downsample[n] contains the downsample factor of level n.

Metadata about the image.

This is a map: property name -> property value.

documentation:
Images associated with this whole-slide image.

This is a map: image name -> PIL.Image.

methods.name: openslide.AbstractSlide.color_profile
documentation: Color profile for the whole-slide image, or None if unavailable.

methods.name: openslide.AbstractSlide.get_best_level_for_downsample
documentation: Return the best level for displaying the given downsample.

methods.name: openslide.AbstractSlide.read_region
documentation:
Return a PIL.Image containing the contents of the region.

location: (x, y) tuple giving the top left pixel in the level 0
          reference frame.
level:    the level number.
size:     (width, height) tuple giving the region size.

methods.name: openslide.AbstractSlide.set_cache
documentation:
Use the specified cache to store recently decoded slide tiles.

This class does not support caching, so this method does nothing.

cache: an OpenSlideCache object.

methods.name: openslide.AbstractSlide.get_thumbnail
documentation:
Return a PIL.Image containing an RGB thumbnail of the image.

size:     the maximum size of the thumbnail.
The base class of a slide object.

methods.name: openslide.OpenSlide.__init__
documentation: Open a whole-slide image.

methods.name: openslide.OpenSlide.detect_format
documentation:
Return a string describing the format vendor of the specified file.

If the file format is not recognized, return None.

methods.name: openslide.OpenSlide.close
documentation: Close the OpenSlide object.

methods.name: openslide.OpenSlide.level_count
documentation: The number of levels in the image.

methods.name: openslide.OpenSlide.level_dimensions
documentation:
A tuple of (width, height) tuples, one for each level of the image.

level_dimensions[n] contains the dimensions of level n.

methods.name: openslide.OpenSlide.level_downsamples
documentation:
A tuple of downsampling factors for each level of the image.

level_downsample[n] contains the downsample factor of level n.

methods.name: openslide.OpenSlide.properties
documentation:
Metadata about the image.

This is a map: property name -> property value.

methods.name: openslide.OpenSlide.associated_images
documentation:
Images associated with this whole-slide image.

This is a map: image name -> PIL.Image.

Unlike in the C interface, the images accessible via this property
are not premultiplied.

methods.name: openslide.OpenSlide.get_best_level_for_downsample
documentation: Return the best level for displaying the given downsample.

methods.name: openslide.OpenSlide.read_region
documentation:
Return a PIL.Image containing the contents of the region.

location: (x, y) tuple giving the top left pixel in the level 0
          reference frame.
level:    the level number.
size:     (width, height) tuple giving the region size.

Unlike in the C interface, the image data returned by this
function is not premultiplied.

methods.name: openslide.OpenSlide.set_cache
documentation:
Use the specified cache to store recently decoded slide tiles.

By default, the object has a private cache with a default size.

cache: an OpenSlideCache object.

An open whole-slide image.

close() is called automatically when the object is deleted.
The object may be used as a context manager, in which case it will be
closed upon exiting the context.

If an operation fails, OpenSlideError is raised.  Note that OpenSlide
has latching error semantics: once OpenSlideError is raised, all future
operations on the OpenSlide object, other than close(), will fail.

classes.name: openslide.OpenSlideCache

methods.name: openslide.OpenSlideCache.__init__
documentation: Create a tile cache with the specified capacity in bytes.

An in-memory tile cache.

Tile caches can be attached to one or more OpenSlide objects with
OpenSlide.set_cache() to cache recently-decoded tiles.  By default,
each OpenSlide object has its own cache with a default size.

modules.name: openslide.lowlevel
documentation:
Low-level interface to the OpenSlide library.

Most users should use the openslide.OpenSlide class rather than this
module.

This module provides nearly direct equivalents to the OpenSlide C API.
(As an implementation detail, conversion of premultiplied image data
returned by OpenSlide into a non-premultiplied PIL.Image happens here
rather than in the high-level interface.)
openslide-python - Python bindings for the OpenSlide library

Copyright (c) 2016-2024 Benjamin Gilbert

classes.name: openslide.lowlevel.OpenSlideError
documentation:
An error produced by the OpenSlide library.

Import this from openslide rather than from openslide.lowlevel.

classes.name: openslide.lowlevel.OpenSlideVersionError
documentation:
This version of OpenSlide does not support the requested functionality.

Import this from openslide rather than from openslide.lowlevel.

classes.name: openslide.lowlevel.OpenSlideUnsupportedFormatError
documentation:
OpenSlide does not support the requested file.

Import this from openslide rather than from openslide.lowlevel.
